policies.title: Xecurify Code of Conduct
policies.text:
CODE OF CONDUCT
This purpose includes its clients and customers and is achieved through the demonstration of the Xecurify and affiliates' values by those who interact with them.

A. Unity:
We acknowledge and uphold the inherent dignity of all individuals with whom we engage, treating them with respect and valuing their contributions.
We are collectively aligned in pursuit of a common objective, fostering cooperation and shared purpose.
We are committed to promoting diversity and ensuring inclusion, striving to create an environment where all individuals are valued and afforded equal opportunities.

B. Agility:
We undertake actions with promptness and make decisions in a timely and decisive manner.
We demonstrate flexibility, responsiveness, and a proactive approach to change and evolving circumstances.
We exercise prudent judgment in taking calculated risks when necessary, in alignment with organizational objectives.

C. Energy:
We are committed to driving positive change and continuously challenging established norms and practices.
We prioritize and support the development of individual leadership capabilities.
We foster a motivating environment by encouraging mutual ambition and inspiring one another to achieve collective goals.

D. Accountability:
We are committed to providing the highest possible value to our customers and stakeholders, ensuring their interests are met and expectations are exceeded.
We accept full responsibility for our actions and outcomes, maintaining accountability for the results we deliver.
We recognize and reward performance, acknowledging achievements in accordance with established criteria and organizational standards.

Accordingly, Xecurify encourages its customers to partner with it in achieving this goal, by ensuring that all their employees and subcontractors interact with Xecurify's personnel, clients, and customers on Xecurify's behalf, and demonstrate these values and behaviors.

This Code of Conduct is designed to ensure that customers align with Xecurify's philosophy in these areas and to encourage their adherence to the principles and values upheld by Xecurify.

E. Xecurify upholds the following principles and expects its customers to comply with them:
To comply with all applicable laws and regulations of the countries in which it operates
To support and respect the protection of internationally proclaimed human rights.
Not to be complicit in human rights abuses.
To uphold the freedom of association and the effective recognition of the right to collective bargaining.
Not to make use of any forms of forced and compulsory labor or child labor.
To prevent discrimination in respect of employment and occupation.
To support a precautionary approach to environmental challenges.
To undertake initiatives to promote greater environmental responsibility.
To encourage the development and diffusion of environmentally friendly technologies.
To work against corruption in all its forms, including extortion and bribery.

F. Xecurify intends to make these principles key drivers of its purchasing policy:

1. Compliance with applicable laws and regulations
Xecurify adheres to the principle that its business should be conducted in accordance with all applicable laws of the countries where it conducts its businesses and any other applicable foreign jurisdictions.

2. Environment, Social, and Governance (ESG)
Xecurify' ESG mission is to:
Deliver a high integrity ecosystem, for all our global stakeholders and our community, thereby enhancing the security and transparency of Xecurify's markets.
Respect and develop our human capital, which is critical to our business success, and equip our employees with appropriate Human Resources policies.
Drive and facilitate sustainable finance, foster dialogue between the different players in the industry and contribute to the financing of the real economy in its transition to a sustainable society.

3. Human Rights
Xecurify is strongly committed to the protection of human rights globally and is guided by fundamental principles, including those enshrined in the United Nations Universal Declaration of Human Rights, the International Labour Organisation (ILO) Core Conventions, and applicable provisions under Indian law.

4. Modern Slavery Act Statement
Xecurify Inc. and its subsidiaries and affiliates are committed to ensuring that there is no modern slavery or human trafficking in our supply chains or in any part of our business.

5. Diversity and Inclusion
Xecurify has a firm commitment to enabling equality of opportunity and workplace cultures that promote inclusion.

6. Business Integrity
Xecurify is fully committed to conducting its operations in strict compliance with all applicable laws, regulations, and standards, including Anti-Money Laundering (AML) Compliance, Sanctions Compliance, Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption, and Intellectual Property Protection.

7. Reputation
Xecurify shall not engage in any conduct that may tarnish or harm the reputation or image of any company within the Xecurify group.

8. Customer's Acknowledgment
By entering into a customer relationship with Xecurify and purchasing products and/or services from Xecurify, the customer acknowledges and agrees to adhere to the principles and philosophy set forth in Xecurify's Code of Conduct throughout the duration of the relationship.

policies.title: Data Retention Policy
policies.text:
Data Retention
Last updated: September 16, 2025

Purpose
This Data Retention Policy will set out for which data is to be retained, the criteria for establishing and reviewing such period(s), and when and how it is to be deleted or otherwise disposed of.

The purpose of this Policy is to ensure that necessary records and documents are adequately protected and maintained, and to ensure that records that are no longer needed by miniOrange or are of no value are discarded at the proper time.

In order to provide customers with secure choices, what data we store, how we process the data, and how the data is purged are mentioned in this policy, ensuring compliance with applicable EU (GDPR), DPDPA, and other global privacy regulations.

miniOrange aims to retain only the data necessary to effectively perform its services or activities and fulfill its mission.

Scope
This policy covers all data collected by miniOrange and stored on miniOrange-owned systems and media, regardless of location.

Data Category | Retention Period | Notes or Legal Basis
Account & contact info | Lifetime of customer relationship + 3 years | GDPR storage limitation; DPDPA Section 8
Usage logs/app data | 3 months to 2 years (per contract) | Minimized per operational/security need
Transaction records | 7 years | Tax, accounting, and compliance laws
Support tickets/emails | 2 years after resolution | Business need: GDPR/DPDPA
Consent registrations | 7 years post-withdrawal (DPDPA) | DPDPA requirement
Contractual documents | 7 years post-contract end | Business/legal need
Backup images | 2 years or as contractually agreed | Secure, restricted access
Marketing communications | Until opt-out or withdrawal + 1 year | GDPR/DPDPA; business need

Data Destruction
When the retention period for the data as outlined above expires, miniOrange will actively destroy the data covered by this policy. In rare circumstances, a litigation hold may be issued by legal counsel prohibiting the destruction of certain documents.

Method of Data Deletion and Disposal
Once the applicable retention period expires, customer data will be securely deleted from all production and backup systems (physical: shredding; electronic: secure erasure/cryptographic wiping).

Deletion events are logged for audit and regulatory review purposes.

a. Secure Deletion: Upon termination of the Agreement, completion of the processing purpose, or upon written request from the Disclosing Party, miniOrange shall securely delete or destroy all confidential information and personal data—including all electronic and physical copies—in its possession or control.

b. Physical Data Disposal: All physical records containing confidential or personal information shall be destroyed using cross-cut shredding, incineration, or other appropriate methods.

c. Data in Backups and Archives: Backup copies and archived data must also be securely deleted according to documented retention schedules, and log files of such actions shall be maintained for audit purposes.

d. Data Principal Rights: Customers (data principals) have the right to access, rectify, restrict, or request deletion of their personal data at any time, except where retention is otherwise required by law.

e. Legal Hold and Exceptions: If data is subject to ongoing litigation, audit, or investigation, regular deletion is paused for relevant data until given clearance by the Legal/Compliance team.

f. Cross-Border Transfers: If customer data is transferred internationally, appropriate safeguards (e.g., Standard Contractual Clauses, government approvals under DPDPA) are implemented and documented, and customers are informed beforehand.

Contact: info@xecurify.com | +1 978 658 9387 | https://www.miniorange.com/contact

policies.title: Incident Response Plan
policies.text:
Incident Response Plan
Document No. MO10 | Revision Date: Jan 2, 2025 | Author: Suraj Pandey
Approved by ISO 27001 Annex A: A.16.1.1 through A.16.1.7

1. Policy Overview
This Security Incident Response Policy is formulated in alignment with ISO 27001:2013 and is designed to establish comprehensive measures for the detection, management, and resolution of security vulnerabilities and incidents.

2. Scope
This Policy applies to all individuals who access, use, or interact with information systems managed by miniOrange, including employees, contractors, and external parties.

3. Roles and Responsibilities
The Information Security Officer and senior management team shall be responsible for implementation, maintenance, and oversight of this Policy.

4. Policy
Severity levels:
a. Highest Severity — Level One: Service failure due to environmental threat (natural disasters, terrorist attack, power failure, fire).
b. Medium Severity — Level Two: Service failure depending on third-party suppliers/vendors (AWS Downtime, Google Server Downtime).
c. Low Severity — Level Three: Service unavailable due to incidents in internal network.

Incident types include unauthorized loss/alteration/destruction of data, unauthorized modification to information processing facility, external attacks (phishing, DDoS, malware), and unexpected device malfunction.

Incident Logging
All incidents must be logged before executing the incident response plan. Employees must report any level of incident to top management/concerned team within 24 hours. Customers can report incidents to miniOrange anytime.

5. Communication Plan
Customers will be notified within 8 hrs of the incident or depending on the severity of the incident.

6. Incident Management
Phase 1: Immediate Action — ISMS Team assesses severity.
Phase 2: Testing and Monitoring — document all actions post-incident.
Phase 3: Backup Execution and Post-Incident Meeting — develop follow-up action plan.
Phase 4: Ongoing Assessment — continuous assessment until resolved.

7. Service Plan
miniOrange relies on AWS infrastructure. Incidents in AWS environment addressed per AWS SLA. Google Workspace used for internal communication; telephonic channels as contingency.

8. Generic Plan
Incident Manager empowered to take any action necessary to resolve incident.

9. Responsibilities of Incident Manager
Ensure effective communication internally and externally. Assemble response team. Oversee post-incident report.

10. Records and Reporting
ISMS Team prepares incident report with time of impact, detection, resolution, and lessons learned.

11. Review of Information Security Continuity
Following resolution, Incident Response Plan reviewed to identify deficiencies and implement corrective actions.

12. Contact
HR Team: hr@xecurify.com
Operations: operations@xecurify.com
ISMS Team: info@xecurify.com

policies.title: Security Practices Policy
policies.text:
Security Practices
Last updated: 17th July, 2025

1. Introduction
This Security Practices Policy is established by miniOrange to set forth guidelines, procedures, and responsibilities relating to the protection of the Company's software, systems, intellectual property, and data.

2. Definitions
Sensitive Data means any data protected by legal or regulatory requirements, including personal data, financial information, and proprietary business data.
Employee means any individual employed by the Company.
Third-party vendors means external entities engaged by the Company.
Incident means any event, suspected event, or actual breach of security, confidentiality, or integrity.

3. Platform
The miniOrange platform provides secure foundation for cloud applications. Communications are secure and verified. Additional security measures prevent XSS, forgery requests, and SQL injections.

4. Domain Access
All access uses HTTPS. Customers assigned own domains and sub-domains. Strong encryption for sensitive customer data including SAML keys. Application-level encryption using AES-256 with customer-exclusive symmetric keys.

5. Customer data
miniOrange validates data on every request. User/customer relationship revalidated on every request. Encryption at rest and in transit. Amazon RDS encryption for data-at-rest.

6. Data Encryption Policies
miniOrange uses Amazon KMS for symmetric encryption. Uses RSA, DSA, TRIPLE-DES, AES, and HMAC. Passwords stored using salted bcrypt with high rounds. All sensitive data at rest encrypted using AES-256; data in transit secured through TLS 1.2 or higher.

7. Physical Security Measures
Visitor access controlled. Fire suppression, climate control, UPS, disaster recovery measures.

8. Employee Termination Procedures
All access rights revoked immediately upon termination. Company property returned. Data access logs reviewed after departure.

9. Operations
Technical teams with experience operating on-demand services. AWS partnership for security and scalability.

10. Network
AWS network protects against DDoS, MITM, IP spoofing, port scanning, packet sniffing.

11. High Availability
Deployed on AWS behind WAF and load balancer with auto-scaling. 99.99% uptime target. Regular snapshots and daily database backups.

12. Disaster Management
Public mechanisms for customers to report security/privacy events. Environmental controls and fail-over mechanisms.

13. Secure Development Lifecycle
Security checkpoints from design through deployment. Annual developer security training.

14. Secure Personnel
Security controls govern employees and contractors. Production access limited to those who need it.

15. Vulnerability Protection
Protection against XSS, XSRF, SQL Injection, MitM, DoS, brute-force attacks.

16. Penetration Testing
Uses tools like Netsparker. Design review, source review, penetration testing. Customers may conduct penetration tests in test environments.

17. Server and physical access
Servers monitored twice per minute. AWS data centers with strict physical access controls. Multi-factor authentication for administrative access.

18. Compliance and Audits
Periodic internal and external security audits.

19. Third-Party Vendor Management
All employees, contractors, and vendors must acknowledge compliance with this Policy.

policies.title: Privacy Policy
policies.text:
Privacy Policy
Last updated: January 06, 2025

1. Introduction
This Privacy Policy explains how Xecurify and miniOrange collect, store, use, disclose, and process information relating to individuals (Personal Data). Services include website www.miniorange.com, SaaS services, and customer communication channels (email, call, SMS, WhatsApp).

2. Xecurify's Roles & Responsibilities
When processing Personal Data as processor on behalf of customers, each customer controls what information is submitted. Xecurify processes such data per agreements with the Customer.

3. Information you provide us
We may collect name, email, postal address, phone number when you sign up, submit inquiries, register for events, or request support.
Contract and Payment Data: billing name, address, last four digits of credit card (full card number not stored).
Communications via SMS, Call & WhatsApp for service-related messages.

4. Information We Automatically Collect
Usage Data, IP addresses, browser type, cookies, Service Data including API logs and authorized user activity patterns.

5. Information We Receive from Third Parties
From resellers, business partners, review sites, and publicly available sources.

6. Use of Information
Operate and improve Services; customer support; security and fraud prevention; marketing (per preferences); legal compliance.

7. Sharing of Personal Information
With service providers (cloud hosting, messaging), affiliates, business partners (with consent), and as required by law. We do not sell personal information.

8. Legal Bases for Processing (UK/EEA)
Consent, legitimate interest, contractual necessity, legal obligation.

9. Your Rights
Right to access, rectification, erasure, restriction, data portability, object, and complain to supervisory authority.

10. Data Retention
Retained while ongoing legitimate business need exists. When no longer needed, deleted or anonymized. Subscriber Data retained per Subscriber instructions and applicable law.

11. Security of Personal Information
Technical and organizational measures including encryption, firewalls, intrusion detection, network monitoring. See Security Practices.

12. International Data Transfer
Servers in U.S., Europe, and Australia. Standard Contractual Clauses for EEA/UK transfers.

13. General
DPA available on request. Children's data not knowingly collected under 16. Contact info@xecurify.com for concerns.

14. External links
Third-party sites governed by their own privacy policies.

15. Changes in the Policy
Posted on website with revision date. Significant updates may be communicated via email, SMS, or WhatsApp.

16. Contact
Email: info@xecurify.com | Phone: +1 978 658 9387 | https://www.miniorange.com
